[The Hidden Truth About Cursor's Command Blocking: How I Mastered the Complete Allowlist Configuration in 3 Proven Steps - Dre Dyson](https://dredyson.com/the-hidden-truth-about-cursors-command-blocking-how-i-mastered-the-complete-allowlist-configuration-in-3-proven-steps/)\n2. [Command Allowlist is silently ignored when \"Auto-Run in Sandbox\" is enabled - Support / Bug Reports - Cursor - Community Forum](https://forum.cursor.com/t/command-allowlist-is-silently-ignored-when-auto-run-in-sandbox-is-enabled/152136)\n3. [Agent Sandboxing - available in Cursor 2.0 - Announcements - Cursor - Community Forum](https://forum.cursor.com/t/agent-sandboxing-available-in-cursor-2-0/139449)\n4. [Auto-Run in Agent Mode - Allow all commands feature - Ideas / Feature Requests - Cursor - Community Forum](https://forum.cursor.com/t/auto-run-in-agent-mode-allow-all-commands-feature/83933)\n5. [Stop Dangerous Cursor Auto-Runs in 3 Minutes Flat (Tested Fix) - Dre Dyson](https://dredyson.com/stop-dangerous-cursor-auto-runs-in-3-minutes-flat-tested-fix/)\n\n# Cursor Command Allowlist and Auto-Run Settings for macOS\n\n## Location in settings.json\n\nCommand allowlist settings are configured in Cursor's &#96;settings.json&#96; file. On macOS, you can also access settings through Cursor → Settings → Chat or Agent.[1][5]\n\n## Key Settings for Auto-Run Configuration\n\nTo control auto-run behavior in &#96;settings.json&#96;, use:[1][5]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"security.commandEvaluationMode\": \"fullStatement\",\n  \"security.allowPartialMatches\": false,\n  \"security.suppressPipeWarnings\": true,\n  \"cursor.commandAllowlist\": [\n    \"git status\",\n    \"npm install\",\n    \"echo\"\n  ],\n  \"cursor.allowCommandSubstrings\": false,\n  \"cursor.experimental.terminalTool\": false\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Important Limitation: Auto-Run in Sandbox Mode\n\nThere is a known issue where the command allowlist is **silently ignored when \"Auto-Run in Sandbox\" is enabled**.[2] When sandbox mode is active, commands auto-run without checking the allowlist, which defeats the allowlist's security purpose.[2]\n\n## Recommended Safe Configuration for macOS\n\nTo ensure the allowlist functions properly:[5]\n\n1. Disable auto-run in Cursor → Settings → Chat\n2. Check \"Always ask before running commands\"\n3. Set &#96;\"cursor.experimental.terminalTool\": false&#96; to use legacy terminal mode\n4. Populate your allowlist with only the specific commands you want to auto-run\n\n## macOS-Specific Considerations\n\nmacOS users running on ARM (M1/M2) may experience more command blocking issues than other platforms.[1] Cursor 2.0+ on macOS uses macOS Seatbelt kernel-level sandboxing for security, which restricts commands from network access and file writes outside your workspace and &#96;/tmp&#96;.[3]",

_**Agent (premium)**_

Two likely reasons, and one is definitely visible in your file:

1. **`git push` is not in your allowlist**
   - Your current `.cursor/config.json` includes `git add`, `git commit`, `git status`, `git diff`, but **not `git push`**.

2. **Allowlists usually match exact command strings**
   - If Cursor runs `git add .`, `git commit -m "..."`, or chained commands like `git add . && git commit ...`, those often **don’t match** a plain `git add` / `git commit` entry.

So even with `@config.json`, you can still get prompts.

If you want, I can update it now to use broader entries (for example `git *`) and explicitly include `git push` so approvals stop for normal git workflows.

_**User**_

how to get to allowlist?

---

_**Agent (premium)**_

Quickest way:

- Open **Cursor Settings** (`Cmd+,`)
- Search for **allowlist** or **allowedCommands**
- Edit at project level via `.cursor/config.json` (your repo already has this)

For your current project, the allowlist file is:
- `.cursor/config.json`

Global (all projects) settings file is:
- `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/settings.json`

If you want, I can set both project + global to the same allowlist so behavior is consistent everywhere.

---

_**User**_

i need ffprobe and ffmpeg installed on my mac, how?

---

_**Agent (premium)**_

Best way on macOS is Homebrew.

### Install
```bash
brew install ffmpeg
```

That installs both `ffmpeg` and `ffprobe`.

### Verify
```bash
ffmpeg -version
ffprobe -version
which ffmpeg
which ffprobe
```

### If `brew` is missing
Install Homebrew first:
```bash
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
```
Then run:
```bash
brew install ffmpeg
```

### Optional (if command not found after install)
For Apple Silicon Macs:
```bash
echo 'eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"' >> ~/.zprofile
eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
```

If you want, I can run these checks for you in this repo terminal and confirm everything is working.
